story-context.title: Redis Revocation Cache (Layer 7)
story-context.summary: Implement JWT Layer 7 (Token Revocation) with Redis blacklist cache, configurable fail-closed/fail-open behavior for Redis unavailability, and admin-only revocation API endpoint (POST /auth/revoke). This story enables immediate token invalidation for security incidents (compromised tokens, user logout) while balancing security vs. availability trade-offs.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: RedisRevocationStore.kt implements revocation check and storage
Layer 7: Revocation validation queries Redis for token JTI (JWT ID)
Revoked tokens stored with 10-minute TTL (matching token lifetime)
Integration test validates: revoke token → subsequent requests rejected with 401
Redis unavailable fallback configurable (fail-open default, fail-closed optional)
Integration test validates both modes (fail-open graceful degradation, fail-closed SecurityException)

critical-architectural-decision.decision: Fail-Open vs. Fail-Closed for Redis Unavailability
critical-architectural-decision.rationale:
**Default: Fail-Open (fail-closed=false)** - Prioritizes availability over security - Suitable for: Development, low-risk environments - Behavior: Redis unavailable → skip check, log warning, allow request - Risk: Revoked tokens accepted during Redis outage - Mitigation: Monitor Redis health, alert on failures
**Optional: Fail-Closed (fail-closed=true)** - Prioritizes security over availability - Suitable for: Production, high-risk environments - Behavior: Redis unavailable → throw SecurityException, reject request with 401 - Requirement: Redis HA (Sentinel/Cluster)

example.name: RedisRevocationStore.kt
example.description: CRITICAL: Token revocation with configurable fail-closed/fail-open behavior

example.name: AuthController.kt

test-scenario.name: Happy Path
test-scenario.step: Admin calls POST /auth/revoke with valid JWT
Verify token stored in Redis with TTL
Subsequent API request with same JWT → 401 Unauthorized

test-scenario.name: Fail-Open Mode (default)
test-scenario.step: API request with revoked token → 200 OK (graceful degradation)

common-pitfalls.pitfall: Not handling Redis connection failures gracefully (crashes instead of degrading)
TTL not set → tokens remain revoked forever (Redis memory leak)
JTI (JWT ID claim) missing in JWT → NPE in revoke()
Fail-closed mode without Redis HA → complete service outage

definition-of-done.item: Integration tests pass for both modes
Revocation API returns 204 No Content on success
